member.name: T:LiteDbX.ILiteCollection`1
member.summary:
Async-only contract for a typed document collection.
Query composition methods such as and the native query builder entrypoints remain synchronous because they build expression trees without touching storage. All operations that read from or write to storage are async.

member.name: T:LiteDbX.ILiteDatabase
member.summary:
Async-only public database contract.
All data operations, maintenance, and schema management are async. The former ambient per-thread transaction model (BeginTrans, Commit, Rollback) has been replaced with explicit scope objects via .
Lifecycle: - Open/create the database with a factory or builder (implementation detail deferred to later phases). - Use await using for deterministic async disposal.
Phase 2 (Transactions and Locking) will implement the transaction scope association. Phase 3 (Disk) will make open/close/checkpoint genuinely async.

member.summary: The instance used by this database.

member.summary: User-defined database schema version number.

member.summary: Timeout used when waiting for lock acquisition.

member.summary: When true, dates are returned in UTC; otherwise local time.

member.summary: Maximum allowed data file size in bytes.

member.summary: Number of WAL pages that trigger an auto-checkpoint. Use 0 for manual-only checkpointing. Default: 1000.

member.summary: Read-only collation used by this database (changeable only via rebuild).

member.name: T:LiteDbX.ILiteQueryable`1
member.summary:
Fluent async query builder for a typed collection.
Composition methods are synchronous (no I/O; they build an expression plan). Execution is triggered by the terminal operations defined on , all of which are async-only.

member.name: T:LiteDbX.ILiteQueryableResult`1
member.summary:
Terminal execution surface for a composed query.
Paging and hint methods are synchronous (pure plan modification). All materialising and streaming operations are async-only — they hit storage.

Async-only repository convenience wrapper.

member.name: T:LiteDbX.LiteDbXQueryableAsyncExtensions
member.summary:
Async terminal extensions for provider-backed LiteDbX queries.
These extensions preserve the Phase 1 contract: synchronous LINQ composition with async-only execution. Each terminal lowers the provider-backed query into a fresh native / and then delegates to the existing LiteDbX query engine path.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.LiteDbXQueryableAsyncExtensions.GetPlanAsync``1(System.Linq.IQueryable{``0},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Execute the translated provider-backed query in explain-plan mode and return the native execution plan document. This preserves query-plan visibility without requiring callers to abandon the LINQ surface prematurely.

member.name: T:LiteDbX.LiteDatabase
member.summary:
The LiteDBX database. Used for creating a LiteDBX instance and using all storage resources.
Phase 4: Updated to implement . All data operations, transactions, maintenance, and SQL execution are now async-only. The former synchronous ambient-transaction API (BeginTrans/Commit/Rollback) is removed; use and the returned instead.

member.name: T:LiteDbX.LiteQueryable`1
member.summary:
Fluent async query builder for a typed collection.
Composition methods (Where, OrderBy, Include, Select, Offset, Limit, ForUpdate, GroupBy, Having) are synchronous — they only mutate an in-memory plan with no I/O.
Terminal operations (ToEnumerable, ToList, ToArray, First, FirstOrDefault, Single, SingleOrDefault, Count, LongCount, Exists, Into, ExecuteReader, GetPlan) are async-only.
Phase 4: all terminal methods replaced with genuine async implementations. The sync bridge (ExecuteReader(), ToList(), etc.) has been removed.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.LiteQueryable`1.ExecuteReader(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Return an async cursor over the raw result set. The cursor wraps the returned by the engine so that field-level access and collection context are preserved on the interface.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.LiteQueryable`1.ToDocuments(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Stream results as values.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.LiteQueryable`1.ToEnumerable(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Stream results as values, applying the BsonMapper. For simple types (string, int, etc.) the first field of each result document is used.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.LiteQueryable`1.Into(System.String,LiteDbX.BsonAutoId,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary:
Insert all results into . Returns the number of documents inserted.
The Into logic runs inside : documents are buffered, then inserted under a separate auto-transaction, and a single { count: N } document is yielded back.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.LiteQueryable`1.GetPlan(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Execute the query with ExplainPlan = true and return the execution plan document. The ExplainPlan flag is reset to false after execution.

member.name: T:LiteDbX.BsonMapper
member.summary: Class that converts your entity class to/from BsonDocument If you prefer use a new instance of BsonMapper (not Global), be sure cache this instance for better performance Serialization rules: - Classes must be "public" with a public constructor (without parameters) - Properties must have public getter (can be read-only) - Entity class must have Id property, [ClassName]Id property or [BsonId] attribute - No circular references - Fields are not valid - IList, Array supports - IDictionary supports (Key must be a simple datatype - converted by ChangeType)

member.name: T:LiteDbX.LockFileEngine
member.summary:
Cross-process engine wrapper that coordinates write operations through an exclusive lock file and keeps the underlying open only for the duration of each outermost call.
Reads use a short-lived engine as well, so writer handles are not kept open between calls. Nested operations in the same async flow reuse the same engine lease. Explicit multi-call transactions remain unsupported for the same reason as : there is no durable ambient engine scope across arbitrary user code.

member.name: T:LiteDbX.SharedDataReader
member.summary:
Wraps an and invokes a callback when the reader is disposed. Used by to release the inter-process mutex after query results have been fully consumed.
Phase 4: Updated to implement the async contract ( returning ValueTask<bool>, ).
Phase 6: now uses instead of the blocking OS Mutex. The async dispose callback correctly releases the semaphore via without blocking threads.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.SharedDataReader.#ctor(LiteDbX.IBsonDataReader,System.Func{System.Threading.Tasks.ValueTask})
member.summary: Construct a with an async dispose callback.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.SharedDataReader.#ctor(LiteDbX.IBsonDataReader,System.Action)
member.summary:
Convenience constructor for callers that have a synchronous dispose action. The action is wrapped in a completed .
Phase 6 bridge: synchronous action callers (SharedEngine) use this overload until the mutex release is redesigned to be async.

member.name: T:LiteDbX.SharedEngine
member.summary:
Shared-mode engine wrapper that serialises concurrent access within a single process via an async-safe .
Phase 6 redesign decision:
The previous implementation used a named OS with a blocking WaitOne() call, which violates the async-only architecture rule that no blocking waits may exist in operational paths.
The has been replaced with a SemaphoreSlim(1,1) whose is used in all operational paths, eliminating thread-blocking from the async runtime path.
Cross-process exclusive file coordination (the original named-mutex purpose) is explicitly deferred. No hidden sync fallback is left in place. A future phase may introduce async-safe cross-process coordination (e.g. a polling file-lock strategy using async Task.Delay retries).
remains unsupported: the shared-mode redesign now supports reentrant nested single-call operations within the same async flow, but explicit transaction scope across arbitrary user code still requires a deeper lifecycle redesign.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.SharedEngine.DisposeAsync
member.summary: Phase 6: respects the active shared-session lease and only performs immediate engine disposal when no session is currently pinned.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.SharedEngine.AcquireLeaseAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary:
Acquire a shared-mode lease.
The outermost lease waits on the per-instance gate, opens the engine, and publishes an ambient async-flow context. Nested operations in the same logical async flow reuse the same open engine without waiting on the gate again, avoiding self-deadlock during streaming enumeration.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.SharedEngine.BeginTransaction(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary:
Explicit multi-call transaction scope remains unsupported.
The reentrant shared-session lease model supports nested single-call operations and streaming enumeration in the same async flow, but it does not expose an explicit transaction scope across arbitrary user code.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.SharedEngine.Query(System.String,LiteDbX.Query,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary:
Materialises query results under the shared-session lease, then releases the lease before yielding to the caller.
This preserves seamless nested operations inside the consumer's await foreach body. An ambient context set inside the producer iterator is not guaranteed to be visible in the consumer body, so shared-mode queries must not rely on reentrant gate ownership across the iterator boundary.

member.name: T:LiteDbX.SqlParser
member.summary:
Internal class to parse and execute sql-like commands.
Phase 4: now returns ValueTask<IBsonDataReader> and accepts a . The collation is pre-resolved by the caller (e.g. ) so that the lazy sync Pragma call is replaced by an async await before SqlParser construction.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.SqlParser.ParseCheckpoint(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: CHECKPOINT

member.name: T:LiteDbX.ILiteFileHandle`1
member.summary:
An async-only handle to an open file entry in LiteDB file storage.
Replaces the synchronous LiteFileStream<TFileId> (which inherited ) in the public-facing contract. The underlying Stream type is not exposed because it carries obligatory synchronous members (Read, Write, Flush) that violate the async-only contract.
Phase 5 (File Storage) is responsible for providing the concrete implementation.

member.summary:
Async-only file storage contract.
The synchronous LiteFileStream<TFileId> : Stream is no longer part of the public API because carries obligatory synchronous members that violate the async-only contract. It is replaced by , which exposes only async read/write/flush/seek operations.

member.name: T:LiteDbX.LiteFileHandle`1
member.summary:
Async-only implementation of .
Replaces the former LiteFileStream<TFileId> : Stream. is not part of the public surface because its abstract contract mandates synchronous Read, Write, and Flush overloads which violate the async-only design rule.
Design notes — Phase 5: ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── • Chunk size: 255 KB (same as the GridFS-derived constant used previously). • Read mode: chunks are loaded on demand asynchronously when is called. No chunk data is prefetched at construction time; the first Read() triggers the first fetch. • Write mode: incoming bytes are buffered in a (CPU-only, no I/O). Once the buffer reaches a chunk is persisted to the collection. Remaining buffered bytes are flushed and file metadata is persisted when is called or when the handle is disposed via . • Seek: supported for read handles only. It is a pure position-state change (O(1)). The chunk that contains the new position is loaded lazily on the next . Seek is not supported on write handles (throws ). • Finalization safety: calls exactly once, making it safe to dispose without an explicit flush. Double-dispose is a no-op. • Thread safety: this class is NOT thread-safe. Callers must not share a handle across concurrent async operations. ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

member.summary: Maximum number of bytes stored in a single chunk document (255 KB, same as GridFS default).

member.summary: Binary data of the currently loaded chunk, or null when no chunk is loaded.

member.summary: Zero-based index of the currently loaded chunk, or -1 when no chunk is loaded.

member.summary: Byte offset within for the next read.

member.summary: Lengths of already-loaded chunks, keyed by chunk index. Used for accurate seek calculation.

member.name: T:LiteDbX.LiteFileInfo`1
member.summary:
Metadata record for a file entry stored in LiteDB file storage.
Phase 5 redesign notes: ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── This class is now a plain data transfer object (POCO/DTO). The former synchronous helper methods (OpenRead, OpenWrite, CopyTo, SaveAs) and the injected collection references (_files, _chunks, _fileId) have been removed because:
1. They required injecting references into a data object, creating a hidden dependency that is incompatible with the async-only contract. 2. The operations those methods performed (open handles, copy data to a stream, save to disk) are now exposed as async methods on and , which is the correct home for them in the redesigned API. ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

member.name: T:LiteDbX.LiteStorage`1
member.summary:
Async-only implementation of .
Phase 5 redesign notes: ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── • All operations are genuinely async: chunk lookups, inserts, deletes, and metadata persistence use the async methods that were established in Phases 1–4. • The sync LiteFileStream<TFileId> : Stream is no longer used. Read/write access is provided by via . • Upload helpers ( and the filesystem overload) copy data through an async pipeline: a write handle is opened, bytes are read from the source stream with ReadAsync, and written to the handle with its async Write. • Download helpers drain a read handle into the destination stream via async chunk reads. • File metadata is persisted (upserted) by during its Flush / DisposeAsync. Callers do not need to perform a separate metadata write after upload. • No sync public methods remain. ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

member.name: T:LiteDbX.BsonDataReader
member.summary:
Reads a void, single, or async-stream sequence of results.
Phase 4 redesign: replaced the synchronous + EngineState bridge with an source. now genuinely awaits the enumerator; the former ReadSync internal bridge and sync Dispose have been removed.
Result abstraction decision (Phase 1 / Phase 4): IBsonDataReader is retained as an explicit async cursor (rather than a raw IAsyncEnumerable<BsonDocument>) because it carries context required by SQL-level Execute callers and supports field-level access via this[string field].
Disposal model: For async-stream instances awaits the enumerator's own DisposeAsync, which propagates resource release up the pipeline (e.g. releasing the query transaction held by ). For single-value or empty instances disposal is a no-op ValueTask.

member.summary: Initialize with no value (void result).

member.summary: Initialize with a single pre-computed value.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.BsonDataReader.#ctor(System.Collections.Generic.IAsyncEnumerable{LiteDbX.BsonDocument},System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary:
Initialize with an async document stream.
The provided is bound to the enumerator at construction via so the same token governs both advancing and cancelling the stream.
is set optimistically to true; callers must to discover emptiness, consistent with a forward-only cursor model.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.BsonDataReader.Read(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary:
Advance the cursor to the next result.
For async-stream instances, genuinely awaits the underlying . For single-value instances, returns a completed . The parameter is accepted for interface compatibility; for async-stream instances the token is already bound to the enumerator at construction time.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.BsonDataReaderExtensions.SingleOrDefault(LiteDbX.IBsonDataReader,System.Threading.CancellationToken)

member.name: T:LiteDbX.IBsonDataReader
member.summary:
An async cursor over a BsonValue result set.
Design choice (Phase 1): retained as an explicit cursor rather than replaced with IAsyncEnumerable<BsonDocument> because:
It carries context that is required by SQL-level Execute callers.
It supports indexed field access via this[string field].
It maps cleanly to the cursor-based reader pattern familiar to database client consumers.
Callers can obtain an IAsyncEnumerable<BsonValue> via the extension method.
Phase 4 (Query Pipeline) is responsible for updating the concrete BsonDataReader implementation.

member.summary: Returns true if the result set contains at least one value.

member.summary: Advance the cursor to the next result. Returns true if a value is available in ; false at end of stream.

member.name: T:LiteDbX.Engine.DiskReader
member.summary:
Memory file reader — must call Dispose/DisposeAsync after use to return reader streams into the pool. This class is not ThreadSafe — must have 1 instance per async execution context (obtained from ).
Phase 3: delegates to the async MemoryCache page-load methods so that cache misses issue genuine async stream reads rather than blocking a thread.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.DiskReader.Dispose
member.summary: When dispose, return stream to pool (synchronous path).

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.DiskReader.DisposeAsync
member.summary: Async dispose — returns streams to their pools. Stream return itself is non-blocking so this completes synchronously; the method exists for composability with IAsyncDisposable disposal chains.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.DiskReader.ReadPageAsync(System.Int64,System.Boolean,LiteDbX.Engine.FileOrigin,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Read a page asynchronously. On a cache miss, the page bytes are loaded from the underlying stream using so no thread-pool thread is blocked for I/O.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.DiskReader.NewPage
member.summary: Request for a empty, writable non-linked page (same as DiskService.NewPage)

member.name: T:LiteDbX.Engine.DiskService
member.summary:
Implement custom fast/in memory mapped disk access. [ThreadSafe]
Phase 3 changes:
is now async; lock(stream) replaced with so the caller is never blocked on a thread.
is now async using and .
added as the async enumeration path for operational runtime reads (checkpoint, restore-index bootstrap path kept sync via ).
replaces the sync version for the error-close path.
Implements for the engine async-close lifecycle.

member.name: F:LiteDbX.Engine.DiskService._writerGate
member.summary: Serialises all concurrent WAL writes so that page positions are assigned atomically and written in order. Replaces the former lock(stream) block.

member.name: P:LiteDbX.Engine.DiskService.Cache
member.summary: Get memory cache instance

member.name: P:LiteDbX.Engine.DiskService.MAX_ITEMS_COUNT
member.summary: Maximum number of items (documents or IndexNodes) this database can have. Used to prevent infinite loops in case of pointer problems.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.DiskService.DisposeAsync
member.summary: Async dispose — preferred on the engine shutdown path.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.DiskService.Initialize(System.IO.Stream,LiteDbX.Collation,System.Int64)
member.summary: Create a new empty database (synchronous; called only once on first open).

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.DiskService.GetReader
member.summary: Get a new instance for reading data/log pages. The instance is NOT thread-safe — one per async execution context (transaction).

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.DiskService.WriteLogDisk(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{LiteDbX.Engine.PageBuffer},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Write all pages to the log file. Uses a rather than lock so the caller never blocks a thread-pool thread while waiting for a concurrent write to finish.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.DiskService.WriteLogDiskSync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{LiteDbX.Engine.PageBuffer})
member.summary: Phase 4 bridge: synchronous WAL write for code paths not yet converted to async. All new code should use .

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.DiskService.WriteDataDisk(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{LiteDbX.Engine.PageBuffer},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Write pages DIRECTLY to the data file (checkpoint path). Pages are not cached. Uses and an async flush to avoid blocking.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.DiskService.ReadFull(LiteDbX.Engine.FileOrigin)
member.summary: Read all database pages from sequentially with no cache. Kept synchronous for the engine startup / RestoreIndex path which runs before the async lifecycle is active. Use in all runtime paths.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.DiskService.ReadFullAsync(LiteDbX.Engine.FileOrigin,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Asynchronously enumerate all pages in the given file origin. Pages are streamed with no cache involvement; PageBuffer instances must NOT be Release()'d by the caller because they are not cache-tracked. Used by the checkpoint and WAL-index restore paths.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.DiskService.MarkAsInvalidState
member.summary: Mark a file with a single signal so the next open triggers auto-rebuild. Used on abnormal close; sync version kept for the sync Close path.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.DiskService.MarkAsInvalidStateAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Async version of for the async-close path.

member.name: T:LiteDbX.Engine.MemoryCache
member.summary: Manage linear memory segments to avoid re-creating array buffer in heap memory Do not share same memory store with different files [ThreadSafe]

member.name: F:LiteDbX.Engine.MemoryCache._free
member.summary: Contains free ready-to-use pages in memory - All pages here MUST have ShareCounter = 0 - All pages here MUST have Position = MaxValue

member.name: F:LiteDbX.Engine.MemoryCache._readable
member.summary: Contains only clean pages (from both data/log file) - support page concurrency use - MUST have defined Origin and Position - Contains only 1 instance per Position/Origin - Contains only pages with ShareCounter >= 0 * = 0 - Page is available but is not in use by anyone (can be moved into _free list on next Extend()) * >= 1 - Page is in use by 1 or more threads. Page must run "Release" when finished using

member.name: F:LiteDbX.Engine.MemoryCache._segmentSizes
member.summary: Get memory segment sizes

member.name: F:LiteDbX.Engine.MemoryCache._extends
member.summary: Get how many extends were made in this store

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.MemoryCache.GetReadablePage(System.Int64,LiteDbX.Engine.FileOrigin,System.Action{System.Int64,LiteDbX.BufferSlice})
member.summary: Get page from clean cache (readable). If page doesn't exist, create this new page and load data using factory fn

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.MemoryCache.GetReadableKey(System.Int64,LiteDbX.Engine.FileOrigin)
member.summary: Get unique position in dictionary according with origin. Use positive/negative values

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.MemoryCache.GetReadablePageAsync(System.Int64,LiteDbX.Engine.FileOrigin,System.Func{System.Int64,LiteDbX.BufferSlice,System.Threading.Tasks.ValueTask},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary:
Async version of . On a cache miss the page is loaded from disk using the supplied async so that no thread is blocked waiting for I/O.
Concurrency note: two concurrent async operations may both miss the cache for the same position. Both load the page; only one wins the TryAdd race and the duplicate is recycled back to the free list. This is safe because reading the same page position twice is idempotent.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.MemoryCache.GetWritablePageAsync(System.Int64,LiteDbX.Engine.FileOrigin,System.Func{System.Int64,LiteDbX.BufferSlice,System.Threading.Tasks.ValueTask},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Async version of . The writable page always gets its own buffer; if a clean cached copy exists it is block-copied synchronously (CPU only, no I/O). If no cached copy exists the page is loaded from disk using the async .

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.MemoryCache.GetWritablePage(System.Int64,LiteDbX.Engine.FileOrigin,System.Action{System.Int64,LiteDbX.BufferSlice})
member.summary: Request for a writable page - no other can read this page and this page has no reference Writable pages can be MoveToReadable() or DiscardWritable() - but never Released()

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.MemoryCache.NewPage
member.summary: Create new page using an empty buffer block. Mark this page as writable.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.MemoryCache.NewPage(System.Int64,LiteDbX.Engine.FileOrigin)
member.summary: Create new page using an empty buffer block. Mark this page as writable.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.MemoryCache.TryMoveToReadable(LiteDbX.Engine.PageBuffer)
member.summary: Try to move this page to readable list (if not already in readable list) Returns true if it was moved

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.MemoryCache.MoveToReadable(LiteDbX.Engine.PageBuffer)
member.summary: Move a writable page to readable list - if already exists, override content Used after write operation that must mark page as readable because page content was changed This method runs BEFORE send to write disk queue - but new page request must read this new content Returns readable page

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.MemoryCache.DiscardPage(LiteDbX.Engine.PageBuffer)
member.summary: Completely discard a writable page - clean content and move to free list

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.MemoryCache.GetFreePage
member.summary: Get a clean, re-usable page from store. Can extend buffer segments if store is empty

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.MemoryCache.Extend
member.summary: Check if it's possible move readable pages to free list - if not possible, extend memory

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.MemoryCache.ExtendUnderLock
member.summary: Check if it's possible move readable pages to free list - if not possible, extend memory. Must be called inside .

member.name: P:LiteDbX.Engine.MemoryCache.PagesInUse
member.summary: Return how many pages are in use when call this method (ShareCounter != 0).

member.name: P:LiteDbX.Engine.MemoryCache.FreePages
member.summary: Return how many pages are available (completely free)

member.name: P:LiteDbX.Engine.MemoryCache.ExtendSegments
member.summary: Return how many segments are already loaded in memory

member.name: P:LiteDbX.Engine.MemoryCache.ExtendPages
member.summary: Get how many pages this cache extends in memory

member.name: P:LiteDbX.Engine.MemoryCache.ActiveSegments
member.summary: Return the number of currently active backing segments.

member.name: P:LiteDbX.Engine.MemoryCache.RetiredSegments
member.summary: Return the number of retired backing segments.

member.name: P:LiteDbX.Engine.MemoryCache.WritablePages
member.summary: Get how many pages are used as Writable at this moment

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.MemoryCache.GetPages
member.summary: Get all readable pages

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.MemoryCache.Clear
member.summary: Clean all cache memory - moving back all readable pages into free list This command must be called inside an exclusive lock

member.name: T:LiteDbX.Engine.MemoryCacheConfig
member.summary: Controls optional trimming behavior for . Defaults preserve the current LiteDbX behavior by keeping trimming disabled.

member.name: P:LiteDbX.Engine.MemoryCacheConfig.Enabled
member.summary: Enable opportunistic cleanup of fully free/idle memory segments. Disabled by default so existing applications keep the current cache behavior.

member.name: P:LiteDbX.Engine.MemoryCacheConfig.MaxFreePages
member.summary: Soft threshold for how many free pages can accumulate before cleanup is considered. Cleanup still retires memory only when an entire segment is reclaimable.

member.name: P:LiteDbX.Engine.MemoryCacheConfig.MaxIdleTime
member.summary: Minimum idle time for a fully free segment before it becomes a retirement candidate.

member.name: P:LiteDbX.Engine.MemoryCacheConfig.BatchSize
member.summary: Maximum number of reclaimable segments to process in a single cleanup pass.

member.name: P:LiteDbX.Engine.MemoryCacheConfig.CleanupInterval
member.summary: Minimum time between cleanup passes.

member.name: T:LiteDbX.Engine.PageBufferManager
member.summary: Tracks page-buffer segments so cache cleanup can retire whole idle segments safely. A segment is eligible for retirement only when every page slice from the segment is back in the free queue and the segment has been idle long enough.

member.name: T:LiteDbX.Engine.FileStreamFactory
member.summary:
FileStream disk implementation of disk factory [ThreadSafe]
Phase 3: all FileStream instances are now opened with FileOptions.Asynchronous so that / dispatch genuine OS-level async I/O (IOCP on Windows, io_uring / epoll on Linux) instead of blocking a thread-pool thread.

member.summary: Create new data file FileStream instance based on filename. Opens with FileOptions.Asynchronous (combined with sequential/random hint) so that all read and write operations on the returned stream use async I/O paths.

member.summary: Async-compatible stream acquisition. FileStream construction is synchronous in all current .NET runtimes, so this completes synchronously; however the returned stream uses FileOptions.Asynchronous so its read/write operations are genuinely non-blocking.

member.name: T:LiteDbX.Engine.IStreamFactory
member.summary:
Interface factory to provider new Stream instances for datafile/walfile resources. It's useful to multiple threads can read same datafile
Phase 3: added as the preferred async-first acquisition path. File-stream constructors are inherently synchronous in .NET, so implementations typically complete synchronously; the key benefit is that the returned stream must be opened with FileOptions.Asynchronous so I/O operations on it are genuinely non-blocking.

member.summary: Get new Stream instance (synchronous; used for startup and legacy sync-bridge paths).

member.summary:
Async-first stream acquisition. The returned is opened with FileOptions.Asynchronous where applicable so that / dispatch genuine async I/O rather than blocking a thread-pool thread.
Implementation note: FileStream construction is synchronous in all current .NET runtimes; implementations may return a completed .

Manage multiple open readonly Stream instances from same source (file). Support single writer instance. Close all Stream on dispose. [ThreadSafe]

member.summary: Asynchronously close all Stream instances. Preferred on engine shutdown paths to avoid blocking the calling thread while OS flush operations complete.

member.name: T:LiteDbX.Engine.AesStream
member.summary:
Encrypted AES Stream
Phase 3: and now delegate to the underlying async paths rather than silently falling back to the inherited synchronous default. The underlying must be opened with FileOptions.Asynchronous (guaranteed by ) for these calls to issue genuine OS-level async I/O.
Durability note: CryptoStream.FlushAsync flushes the crypto buffer to the underlying stream; physical-disk durability requires the underlying FileStream.Flush(true) which is still synchronous. This is acceptable on shutdown/checkpoint paths but must not be called on hot async write paths without careful consideration.

member.summary: Decrypt data from Stream

member.summary: Encrypt data to Stream

member.summary: Async decrypt data from Stream. Delegates to which on .NET Core 2.1+ issues genuine async I/O through the underlying stream.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.AesStream.WriteAsync(System.Byte[],System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Async encrypt data to Stream. Delegates to which on .NET Core 2.1+ issues genuine async I/O through the underlying stream.

member.name: T:LiteDbX.Engine.ConcurrentStream
member.summary:
Thread/async-safe stream wrapper that serialises access to a shared underlying stream using a .
Multiple instances wrapping the same underlying stream must share the same so that all callers are mutually exclusive. The gate is provided by which owns the underlying stream.
Phase 3: replaced lock(_stream) with so that and are truly non-blocking.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.ConcurrentStream.#ctor(System.IO.Stream,System.Boolean,System.Threading.SemaphoreSlim)
member.param: Optional semaphore shared with other instances that wrap the same underlying . When null a private gate is created (suitable when this is the only wrapper, e.g. in unit tests).

member.name: T:LiteDbX.Engine.TempStream
member.summary:
Implement a temporary stream that uses MemoryStream until get LIMIT bytes, then copy all to temporary disk file and delete on dispose.
Phase 3: checks the spill threshold and performs the in-memory → file copy asynchronously via , ensuring the hot sort/temp write path does not block a thread-pool thread. is provided so the file deletion can be awaited in async contexts.

member.name: P:LiteDbX.Engine.TempStream.InMemory
member.summary: Indicate that stream are all in memory

member.name: P:LiteDbX.Engine.TempStream.InDisk
member.summary: Indicate that stream is now on disk

member.name: P:LiteDbX.Engine.TempStream.Filename
member.summary: Get temp disk filename (if null will be generated only when creating file)

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.TempStream.WriteAsync(System.Byte[],System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Async write. When the current stream position would exceed and the stream is still in memory, the memory contents are copied to a temporary file asynchronously before the write is dispatched.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.TempStream.DisposeAsync
member.summary: Async dispose — flushes and closes the underlying stream, then deletes any spill file. Uses an runtime check so the code compiles and works correctly on both netstandard2.0 (sync Stream disposal) and .NET 5+ (async FileStream flush).

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.LiteEngine.Query(System.String,LiteDbX.Query,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary:
Run a query over a collection and stream matching documents as an .
Phase 4: the Phase 2/3 bridge notes are retired. now uses — no blocking wait occurs at transaction entry.
Cursor lifetime guarantee: The holds the transaction gate slot for the duration of enumeration. When the caller breaks early, cancels, or fully consumes the sequence the finally block inside releases the cursor registration and returns the transaction to the monitor.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.LiteEngine.Query(System.String,LiteDbX.Query,LiteDbX.Engine.ILiteTransaction,System.Threading.CancellationToken)

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.LiteEngine.Rebuild(LiteDbX.Engine.RebuildOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary:
Rebuild the database fully asynchronously.
Phase 6: uses and so no thread is blocked during the rebuild I/O. Open() after the rebuild still runs synchronously (constructor limitation, deferred to Phase 7 async factory).

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.LiteEngine.Rebuild(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Convenience overload: rebuild using current collation and password settings.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.LiteEngine.RebuildContentAsync(LiteDbX.Engine.IFileReader,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary:
Copy all documents and indexes from into the engine, using the async transaction entry point.
Phase 6: replaces the Phase 3 bridge that used GetOrCreateTransactionSync and EnsureIndex(...).GetAwaiter().GetResult(). All transaction acquisition and index creation are now genuinely awaited.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.LiteEngine.RebuildContent(LiteDbX.Engine.IFileReader)
member.summary:
Synchronous variant of , used exclusively by which is called from the synchronous Recovery() → Open() constructor path.
Phase 6 deferred: still uses GetOrCreateTransactionSync and EnsureIndex(...).GetAwaiter().GetResult() because this code runs inside the synchronous constructor. Resolving this requires the async factory (Phase 7).
Do not call this method from any non-constructor site.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.LiteEngine.Recovery(LiteDbX.Collation)
member.summary:
Recover a corrupt datafile using the rebuild process. Called only from when AutoRebuild = true.
Phase 6 deferred: Recovery() is called synchronously from the Open() / constructor path and therefore cannot be made async without introducing a static async factory method (LiteEngine.OpenAsync()). That factory is deferred to Phase 7.
Uses (internal sync overload) rather than . Do not change this to the async path until the constructor is replaced with an async factory.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.LiteEngine.TryUpgrade
member.summary:
Detect and upgrade a v7 datafile to the current format before the disk service opens.
Phase 6 deferred: TryUpgrade() is called synchronously from Open() (constructor path). The file detection read and the subsequent Recovery() call both run on the calling thread. Replacing this with an async upgrade path requires the Phase 7 async-factory constructor (LiteEngine.OpenAsync()).

member.name: T:LiteDbX.Engine.IFileReader
member.summary:
Interface to read current or old datafile structure — used to shrink/upgrade a datafile from old LiteDB versions.
Phase 6 note: The data-enumeration methods below (, , , ) remain synchronous because they perform bulk sequential reads from a dedicated recovery stream that is isolated from the main engine I/O path. Making them fully async would require streaming the file read through the async disk service, which is a larger refactor scoped to a future phase.
is implemented so the new async rebuild path can use await using for clean resource release. The sync path is retained for the legacy engine-startup recovery that runs inside the synchronous Open() constructor call (deferred to Phase 7 async factory).

member.summary: Get all indexes from collection (except _id index)

member.summary: Get all documents from a collection

member.name: T:LiteDbX.Engine.ILiteEngine
member.summary:
Async-only engine contract.
All data access, maintenance, and schema operations are async. Transactions are represented as explicit scope objects rather than ambient per-thread state; this removes the thread-affinity requirement that is incompatible with await-based continuations.
Phase 2 (Transactions and Locking) is responsible for implementing the association between a transaction scope and engine operations. Phase 3 (Disk and Streams) is responsible for making the underlying I/O actually async.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.ILiteEngine.Checkpoint(System.Threading.CancellationToken)

member.summary: Rebuild the entire database, reclaiming unused pages and optionally re-encrypting. Returns the new file size in bytes.

member.summary: Begin an explicit async transaction. The returned must be committed or rolled back before disposal. Disposing without committing triggers an implicit rollback.

member.name: T:LiteDbX.Engine.LiteTransaction
member.summary:
Concrete implementation of .
Phase 2 — transaction–operation association uses ambient context. Phase 3 — , , and now delegate to and so the WAL write is genuinely async rather than a synchronous bridge.

member.summary: Returns true if an explicit transaction is active in this async context.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.LiteTransaction.DisposeAsync
member.summary: Dispose the transaction. If was not called, performs an implicit rollback. Clears the ambient context for the current async execution flow.

member.summary: Represent page number - start in 0 with HeaderPage [4 bytes]

member.summary: Indicate the page type [1 byte]

member.summary: Represent the next page. Used for page-sequences - MaxValue represent that has NO next page [4 bytes]

member.name: P:LiteDbX.Engine.BasePage.PageListSlot
member.summary: Get/Set where this page are in free list slot [1 byte] Used only in DataPage (0-4) and IndexPage (0-1) - when new or not used: 255 DataPage: 0 (7344 - 8160 free space) - 1 (6120 - 7343) - 2 (4896 - 6119) - 3 (2448 - 4895) - 4 (0 - 2447) IndexPage 0 (1400 - 8160 free bytes) - 1 (0 - 1399 bytes free)

member.summary: Indicate how many items are used inside this page [1 byte]

member.summary: Get how many bytes are used on content area (exclude header and footer blocks) [2 bytes]

member.name: P:LiteDbX.Engine.BasePage.FragmentedBytes
member.summary: Get how many bytes are fragmented inside this page (free blocks inside used blocks) [2 bytes]

member.name: P:LiteDbX.Engine.BasePage.NextFreePosition
member.summary: Get next free position. Starts with 32 (first byte after header) - There is no fragmentation after this [2 bytes]

member.name: P:LiteDbX.Engine.BasePage.HighestIndex
member.summary: Get last (highest) used index slot - use byte.MaxValue for empty [1 byte]

member.summary: Get how many free bytes (including fragmented bytes) are in this page (content space) - Will return 0 bytes if page are full (or with max 255 items)

member.name: P:LiteDbX.Engine.BasePage.FooterSize
member.summary: Get how many bytes are used in footer page at this moment ((HighestIndex + 1) * 4 bytes per slot: [2 for position, 2 for length])

member.name: T:LiteDbX.Engine.QueryExecutor
member.summary:
Executes a and streams matching documents.
Phase 4 redesign:
now returns .
Transaction acquisition uses — the blocking GetOrCreateTransactionSync bridge is no longer used here.
The inner document pipeline () remains synchronous because all its steps are CPU-bound transforms over the in-memory page cache. Async I/O is not required at the per-document level; only the transaction lock entry is a genuine async boundary.
WAL read lock note: still uses EnterReadSync inside CreateSnapshot. Converting that to async is deferred to Phase 3 / Phase 6 (disk and shared-mode redesign).

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.QueryExecutor.ExecuteQuery(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary:
Stream query results as an .
If is set, all source documents are buffered, inserted into the target collection, and a single { count: N } document is yielded. Otherwise matching documents are yielded directly from the CPU pipeline.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.QueryExecutor.ExecuteQueryCore(System.Boolean,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary:
Acquire a transaction (async, non-blocking), execute the synchronous CPU pipeline, and release the transaction when enumeration is complete or abandoned.
Phase 6: is called before the pipeline so that system collections executed as a source (e.g. $dump, $indexes, $page_list) can retrieve the running transaction via instead of the removed thread-local GetThreadTransaction().

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.QueryExecutor.ExecuteQueryInto(System.String,LiteDbX.BsonAutoId,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary:
Execute the query, buffer all source documents, insert them into , then yield a single { count: N } document.
Transaction lifecycle: the query transaction is acquired and fully released inside . The insert runs under a separate auto-transaction via the engine insert path, so the two transactions are sequential, not nested.

member.name: T:LiteDbX.Engine.LockService
member.summary:
Async-safe lock service for collection-based and database-level coordination.
Phase 2 redesign:
replaced with for the transaction gate.
Per-collection Monitor.TryEnter replaced with (1,1) per collection.
All lock-entry methods are async; exit methods remain synchronous (Release() is always safe).
IsInTransaction removed — callers use instead.
Bridge methods (EnterTransactionSync, EnterLockSync) remain for internal paths that have not yet been converted to fully async (QueryExecutor, WalIndexService checkpoint). These will be eliminated in Phase 3/4. [ThreadSafe]

member.name: P:LiteDbX.Engine.LockService.Timeout
member.summary: Exposes the configured lock timeout so cooperating services (e.g. WalIndexService) can use the same timeout value without holding a direct reference to EnginePragmas.

member.name: P:LiteDbX.Engine.LockService.TransactionsCount
member.summary: Returns the number of currently open transactions (approximate; for diagnostics).

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.LockService.EnterTransactionAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Asynchronously acquire a transaction-gate read slot. Multiple transactions can hold this concurrently; exclusive (checkpoint/rebuild) operations block.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.LockService.EnterTransactionSync
member.summary: Phase 3/4 bridge: synchronous transaction-gate entry for code paths not yet converted to async (e.g. QueryExecutor, WalIndexService). Uses .Wait(timeout) which blocks the calling thread.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.LockService.ExitTransaction
member.summary: Release a transaction-gate read slot.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.LockService.EnterLockAsync(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Asynchronously acquire the exclusive write lock for a collection. Only one writer per collection at a time; concurrent readers are blocked by the collection semaphore.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.LockService.EnterLockSync(System.String)
member.summary: Phase 3 bridge: synchronous collection write-lock entry for code paths not yet fully async (e.g. constructor called from legacy sync paths).

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.LockService.ExitLock(System.String)
member.summary: Release the exclusive write lock for a collection.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.LockService.EnterExclusiveAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Asynchronously enter exclusive mode — waits for all open transactions to complete. Used by checkpoint and rebuild operations. Returns true if the lock was acquired (must call afterwards). Returns false if already in exclusive mode.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.LockService.EnterExclusive
member.summary: Phase 3 bridge: synchronous exclusive-lock entry (used by WalIndexService.Checkpoint()).

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.LockService.TryEnterExclusive(System.Boolean@)
member.summary: Try to enter exclusive mode immediately (no wait). Returns false if any readers or another writer currently holds the lock.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.LockService.ExitExclusive
member.summary: Release the exclusive lock.

member.name: T:LiteDbX.Engine.RebuildService
member.summary:
Rebuilds a LiteDB database by reading from the existing file and writing a fresh copy.
Phase 6: is the primary path — it awaits all async engine operations (Pragma, Insert, Checkpoint) without any GetAwaiter().GetResult() calls.
(sync) is retained exclusively for the Recovery() call inside , which runs synchronously from the constructor. Full async engine initialisation (static factory method) is deferred to Phase 7. Do not call from any other site. [ThreadSafe]

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.RebuildService.RebuildAsync(LiteDbX.Engine.RebuildOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Rebuild the database fully asynchronously. All engine calls are awaited. File-system operations (File.Move, File.Exists) remain synchronous because has no built-in async equivalent and runs quickly relative to the overall rebuild duration.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.RebuildService.Rebuild(LiteDbX.Engine.RebuildOptions)
member.summary:
Synchronous rebuild used exclusively by , which is called from the synchronous Open() / constructor path.
Phase 6 deferred: this path calls .GetAwaiter().GetResult() on async engine methods because the constructor cannot yet be made async. The deferred item is creating a static LiteEngine.OpenAsync() factory in Phase 7, which will allow the Recovery path to be converted to .
Do not call this method except from Recovery().

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.RebuildService.SwapFiles(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Renames files to swap the rebuilt temp file into the primary position. Returns the size difference (positive = space reclaimed).

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.RebuildService.ReadFirstBytes(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Read the first 16 KB (2 pages) of the data file for version detection.

member.name: T:LiteDbX.Engine.TransactionMonitor
member.summary:
Tracks and manages all open transactions.
Phase 2 redesign:
ThreadLocal<TransactionService> removed — thread identity is no longer used for ownership.
Explicit transactions are tracked via (AsyncLocal).
is the primary entry point for the async operational path.
is a Phase 4 bridge for internal code not yet converted to async.
[Singleton - ThreadSafe]

member.name: F:LiteDbX.Engine.TransactionMonitor.HeaderCommitGate
member.summary: Serialises concurrent transaction commits that modify the header page. Replaces the former lock(_header) pattern in so that commit operations can await the gate without blocking a thread. Phase 3 addition.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.TransactionMonitor.GetOrCreateTransactionAsync(System.Boolean,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary:
Get or create a transaction for the current async execution context.
If an explicit is ambient (from ), it is reused for non-query operations (isNew = false).
Otherwise a new auto-transaction is created, entered into the transaction gate, and tracked. The caller is responsible for committing and releasing it when isNew is true.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.TransactionMonitor.CreateExplicitTransactionAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Create a new explicit transaction to back a scope. Called exclusively from .

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.TransactionMonitor.GetOrCreateTransactionSync(System.Boolean,System.Boolean@)
member.summary: Phase 4 bridge: synchronous version of for internal code paths not yet converted (e.g. QueryExecutor, RebuildContent). Uses blocking .

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.TransactionMonitor.ReleaseTransaction(LiteDbX.Engine.TransactionService)
member.summary: Release a transaction: dispose it, return its pages to the pool, and exit the transaction gate.

member.name: F:LiteDbX.Engine.TransactionMonitor._currentTransaction
member.summary:
Tracks the that is currently executing on the active async call chain — covers both explicit () and auto-transactions created by .
Phase 6: replaces the old thread-local GetThreadTransaction() pattern (removed in Phase 2) for system collections that need to attach a read snapshot to the transaction that is already running the surrounding query.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.TransactionMonitor.SetCurrentTransaction(LiteDbX.Engine.TransactionService)
member.summary: Set the transaction that is currently driving the async execution context. Called by before the query pipeline runs and cleared (null) after it completes.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.TransactionMonitor.GetCurrentTransaction
member.summary:
Returns the that is currently active on this async execution context, or null if none has been set.
Resolution order:
Explicit ambient transaction ().
Auto-transaction set by .

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.TransactionMonitor.GetAmbientTransaction
member.summary: Legacy alias kept for call sites not yet updated.

member.name: T:LiteDbX.Engine.TransactionService
member.summary:
Manages state and page access for a single transaction.
Phase 2 redesign:
ThreadID property removed — transaction ownership is no longer thread-affine.
added as the Phase 2 async path for snapshot creation.
retained as Phase 3 bridge for legacy sync callers (QueryExecutor).

member.name: P:LiteDbX.Engine.TransactionService.OpenCursors
member.summary: Get/Set how many open cursor this transaction are running

member.name: P:LiteDbX.Engine.TransactionService.ExplicitTransaction
member.summary: Get/Set if this transaction was opened by BeginTrans() method (not by AutoTransaction/Cursor)

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.TransactionService.CreateSnapshot(LiteDbX.Engine.LockMode,System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Create (or get from transaction-cache) snapshot and return. Phase 3 bridge: uses synchronous lock acquisition via . Use for Phase 2+ async callers.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.TransactionService.CreateSnapshotAsync(LiteDbX.Engine.LockMode,System.String,System.Boolean,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Async version of . Acquires the collection write lock asynchronously. This is the Phase 2 primary path; use this from all callers within AutoTransactionAsync.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.TransactionService.Dispose
member.summary: Public implementation of Dispose pattern.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.TransactionService.Safepoint
member.summary: If current transaction contains too much pages, now is safe to remove clean pages from memory and flush to wal disk dirty pages

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.TransactionService.PersistDirtyPages(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Phase 4 bridge: synchronous version of for and the sync bridge. Uses .

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.TransactionService.PersistDirtyPagesAsync(System.Boolean,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary:
Persist all dirty in-memory pages (in all snapshots) and confirm them in the WAL. Returns the number of pages written.
Phase 3: uses (async) and . The caller must hold before calling this method to serialise concurrent commits that modify the header page.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.TransactionService.CommitAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary:
Asynchronously commit the transaction: persist dirty pages, confirm in WAL, dispose snapshots.
Phase 3: replaces lock(_header) with so the commit path never blocks a thread-pool thread.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.TransactionService.Commit
member.summary: Phase 4 bridge: synchronous commit for code paths not yet converted. Prefer from all async callers.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.TransactionService.RollbackAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary:
Asynchronously rollback the transaction: discard dirty pages, return new pages, dispose snapshots.
Phase 3: uses the async WAL write path.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.TransactionService.Rollback
member.summary: Phase 4 bridge: synchronous rollback for paths not yet converted. Prefer from all async callers.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.TransactionService.ReturnNewPagesAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Async: return added pages on rollback by writing them as EmptyPage entries in the log. Phase 3 primary path.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.TransactionService.ReturnNewPages
member.summary: Phase 4 bridge: synchronous return-new-pages for paths not yet converted.

member.name: T:LiteDbX.Engine.WalIndexService
member.summary:
Do all WAL index services based on LOG file - has only single instance per engine. [Singleton - ThreadSafe]
Phase 3 changes:
replaced with so that write-lock acquisition does not block threads in async contexts.
and are now async, awaiting the exclusive lock and calling / .
acquires the index write lock asynchronously.
keeps a sync bridge entry via for the Phase 4 QueryExecutor path.
and keep their sync signatures (called from the sync engine startup path); async versions are provided for Phase 8.

member.name: F:LiteDbX.Engine.WalIndexService._lockTimeout
member.summary: Cached lock timeout, captured from at construction.

member.name: P:LiteDbX.Engine.WalIndexService.CurrentReadVersion
member.summary: Get current read version for all new transactions. Phase 3 bridge: uses to avoid replacing the entire property access pattern in Phase 3.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.WalIndexService.ClearUnderLock
member.summary: Clear WAL index links and cache memory. Must be called while the index write lock is held.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.WalIndexService.Clear
member.summary: Clear WAL index and cache. Acquires the write lock synchronously. Kept for the sync startup/recovery path.

member.name: T:LiteDbX.Engine.SysQuery
member.summary:
$query experimental system collection that allows a sub-query via SQL.
Phase 6 status: explicitly disabled.
Reason: now returns ValueTask<IBsonDataReader> and returns ValueTask<bool>, but must return the synchronous IEnumerable<BsonDocument>. The two contracts are incompatible without migrating the system-collection pipeline to IAsyncEnumerable<BsonDocument>, which is a Phase 7 work item.
Deferred to Phase 7: redesign to support IAsyncEnumerable<BsonDocument>, then re-enable $query using a fully awaited call.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.SysQuery.#ctor(LiteDbX.Engine.ILiteEngine)
member.summary: The parameter is kept for API compatibility with but is not used by the disabled implementation.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.Engine.SysQuery.Input(LiteDbX.BsonValue)
member.summary: Phase 6: always throws . The async SQL execution path cannot be driven from a synchronous IEnumerable source. Re-enable after Phase 7 migrates system collections to IAsyncEnumerable<BsonDocument>.

member.name: T:LiteDbX.AsyncReaderWriterLock
member.summary:
Async-safe readers/writer lock built on .
Multiple readers can hold the lock concurrently. A writer waits for all active readers to release before acquiring exclusive access.
Fairness note: writers can be starved by a continuous stream of new readers. This is acceptable for LiteDB because exclusive locks (checkpoint/rebuild) are rare operations.
Phase 2 — replaces ReaderWriterLockSlim in .

member.name: M:LiteDbX.AsyncReaderWriterLock.EnterReadAsync(System.TimeSpan,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Asynchronously enter read mode. Allows concurrent readers.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.AsyncReaderWriterLock.ExitRead
member.summary: Release the read lock. Can be called synchronously; is always safe. Note: the brief _readerGate.Wait() here protects only an integer decrement — no I/O or awaits occur inside. Phase 3 will eliminate this by ensuring all callers are async-disposable.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.AsyncReaderWriterLock.EnterWriteAsync(System.TimeSpan,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Asynchronously enter exclusive write mode. Waits for all readers to exit first.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.AsyncReaderWriterLock.TryEnterWrite
member.summary: Try to enter write mode immediately without waiting. Returns false if any readers or another writer holds the lock.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.AsyncReaderWriterLock.ExitWrite
member.summary: Release the exclusive write lock.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.AsyncReaderWriterLock.EnterReadSync(System.TimeSpan)
member.summary: Phase 3 bridge: synchronously enter read mode. Blocks the calling thread briefly. Used by WalIndexService.GetPageIndex which is still called from sync paths. Replace callers with when those paths are converted.

member.name: F:LiteDbX.Constants.MEMORY_SEGMENT_SIZES
member.summary: Size, in PAGES, for each buffer array (used in MemoryStore) It's an array to increase after each extend - limited in highest value Each byte array will be created with this size * PAGE_SIZE Use minimal 12 to allocate at least 85Kb per segment (will use LOH)

member.name: M:LiteDbX.IOExceptionExtensions.IsLocked(System.IO.IOException)
member.summary: Returns true if the exception is a file-locking error.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.IOExceptionExtensions.WaitIfLocked(System.IO.IOException,System.Int32)
member.summary:
Blocks the calling thread for ms if the exception is a file-locking error; rethrows for any other exception.
Phase 6: replaced Task.Delay(...).Wait() with Thread.Sleep(). Task.Delay().Wait() misuses the async machinery for a deliberately synchronous delay inside a sync retry loop ( / ). Blocking the thread is intentional in that context — Thread.Sleep is the correct primitive.

member.name: M:LiteDbX.LinqExtensions.IsLast``1(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0})
member.summary: Return same IEnumerable but indicate if item last item in enumerable

member.name: M:LiteDbX.StreamExtensions.FlushToDisk(System.IO.Stream)
member.summary: If Stream are FileStream, flush content direct to disk (avoid OS cache)

member.name: M:LiteDbX.StreamExtensions.FlushToDiskAsync(System.IO.Stream,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary:
Asynchronously flush stream data to the OS buffer (and, on supported platforms, to disk).
Platform note: On .NET 6+ with FileOptions.Asynchronous, FileStream.FlushAsync issues a flush that reaches physical storage on most OS configurations. On older runtimes or non-file streams, this flushes to the OS write buffer but durability at the physical layer is not guaranteed by the async path alone. For hard durability requirements on older platforms, use after the async write completes (acceptable on shutdown paths where blocking is tolerable).
